user_task: Evaluate Retrieval quality for customer datasets.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, EVALUATE bucket; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, EVALUATE -> 'I have N labeled question-answer pairs and an index, tell me how well retrieval is doing, per-query' / 'Run a retrieval benchmark on my corpus and tell me which queries are failing.'

success_criteria.quality: operational pass -- the eval harness consumes a labeled set of (query, gold_pdf_page) or (query, gold_source_ids) pairs against an existing LanceDB index and SURFACES Recall@k (and, via the BEIR path, nDCG@k) as numeric outputs; a per-query pass/fail breakdown at each k is available; the result is reproducible across runs on the same labeled set + index. Binary pass/fail (the metric must appear as a number; this functional row does NOT gate on a metric threshold -- that is the separate performance-eval suite).
success_criteria.time: slow -- <= 10 min per case (build/reuse a small index + embed N queries + search + score).
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% -- an 'evaluate / benchmark retrieval quality on my labeled set' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% -- must use `retriever recall vdb-recall run --query-csv <labeled.csv> --table-name <ingest-table>` for Recall@k (and `retriever ingest` first to build the index); the BEIR path (Recall@k + nDCG@k over an annotations CSV) is reached via the bo767_csv loader / `retriever harness run`. NOT a plain `retriever query` (that returns hits, not metrics).

eval_surface.recall_subapp: `retriever recall` -> sub-app `vdb-recall` -> command `run`: `retriever recall vdb-recall run --query-csv <csv> --table-name <table> [--top-k N] [--lancedb-uri DIR] [--embedding-model M] [--limit N] [--print-hits/--no-print-hits]`. Reads a query ground-truth CSV, embeds each query, searches LanceDB, prints top-k hits per query, and prints Recall@1 / Recall@5 / Recall@10 (the metric ks are FIXED to (1,5,10) inside the command; --top-k only controls how many hits are PRINTED, search_k = max(top_k,10)). Source: nemo_retriever/tools/recall/vdb_recall.py::run + core.py::retrieve_and_score/_recall_at_k.
eval_surface.recall_with_main: `retriever recall vdb-recall recall-with-main` is the same engine with a plainer text print loop (Gold PDF/page + Result lines + Recall@1/5/10); either command surfaces Recall@k.
eval_surface.eval_subapp: `retriever eval` -> `run` (`--config eval_sweep.yaml | --from-env`), `export`, `build-page-index`. `eval run` is a QA-generation sweep (RETRIEVAL_FILE/QA_DATASET/GEN_MODEL/JUDGE_MODEL); the retrieval-quality Recall/nDCG metrics for this row come from the recall sub-app and the BEIR evaluator, not from `eval run`.
eval_surface.ndcg_path: Recall@k AND nDCG@k together come from the BEIR evaluator: nemo_retriever/tools/recall/beir.py::compute_beir_metrics emits both recall@k and ndcg@k for ks (default 1,3,5,10). It is driven by the RecallEvaluatorActor (evaluation_mode='beir') / `retriever harness run --dataset <name>` over a BEIR-style annotations CSV via the bo767_csv loader (load_beir_dataset). A local annotations CSV (columns: modality,query,answer,pdf,page) is accepted by passing its path as the dataset_name to the bo767_csv loader. print_run_summary then prints the BEIR Recall@k/nDCG@k block.
eval_surface.per_query_breakdown: nemo_retriever/tools/recall/core.py::evaluate_recall builds a per-query DataFrame with columns query_id, query, golden_answer, top_retrieved, hit@k (bool pass/fail) and rank@k for each k -- the 'which queries passed/failed at which k' breakdown. `vdb-recall run --print-hits` (default on) also prints per-query Gold + top-k Hits so failures are visible inline.

labeled_set_format.recall_csv: Query ground-truth CSV for `recall vdb-recall run` (pdf_page match mode). Accepts EITHER `query,pdf_page` (e.g. woods_frost_2) OR `query,pdf,page` (page is 1-indexed at the CSV layer; key built = '{pdf}_{page}'). Extra columns (modality, answer, pdf_page) are ignored. Gold key is compared against retrieved keys '{source_stem}_{page_number}' where page_number is the 1-indexed page on the hit. A page value of -1 / '{pdf}_-1' matches the whole document. Source: core.py::_normalize_query_df + _hits_to_keys + _is_hit.
labeled_set_format.beir_annotations_csv: Customer annotations CSV for the BEIR/nDCG path (bo767_csv loader). Columns: modality,query,answer,pdf,page. NOTE the loader does page_number = int(page)+1, i.e. the `page` column is 0-indexed there (so to target 1-indexed page P, write P-1). qrels are built as one relevant doc id per query (graded relevance 1). This is the same shape as the repo's data/bo767_annotations.csv and data/digital_corpora_10k_annotations.csv.

environment.table_name_caveat: DEFAULT TABLE MISMATCH: `retriever ingest` writes table `nemo-retriever` by default, but `retriever recall vdb-recall run` defaults to table `nv-ingest`. Every recall command in this suite MUST pass `--table-name nemo-retriever` (matching the ingest table) or it will read an empty/absent table and report Recall@k = 0 for every query.
environment.embedding: The query embedder defaults to nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2; with no --embedding-endpoint set, recall falls back to local HF embeddings (--local-query-embed-backend hf). Must match the embedder used at ingest time so query and corpus vectors are comparable.
environment.reproducibility: Embedding + vector search are deterministic for a fixed index, query set, and embedder, so Recall@k / nDCG@k are stable across repeated runs on the same labeled set (rung 5 asserts this).

cases.title: Baseline: tiny labeled set (3 queries) over a small index -> Recall@k surfaced as a number
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the EVALUATE operational-pass core (consume a labeled (query, gold_pdf_page) set against an index and surface Recall@k as a NUMBER) in its simplest form: 3 queries over a 2-PDF index. Rung 1 only asserts that Recall@1/@5/@10 print as numeric values for a labeled set -- it does not yet add nDCG or a per-query view. The EVALUATE floor.
cases.prompt: I have a 3-question labeled set in data/labeled_set.csv (query plus the gold pdf and page) and two PDFs in data/. Build an index over the PDFs, then tell me how well retrieval is doing -- give me Recall@k as a number.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 2 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (N >= 2)
expected_output.recall_block: prints 'Recall metrics' then numeric lines recall@1: <x>, recall@5: <y>, recall@10: <z> (each a float in [0,1]); --print-hits (default on) also prints per-query Gold + top-k Hits
expected_output.expected_values: with a correct index, all 3 gold pages are retrievable in the top 5, so recall@5 and recall@10 = 1.0000; recall@1 >= 0.6667 (the woods_frost page-1 poem query and the table cell query land their gold page at rank 1; the 1923-collection query targets woods_frost p2)

pass_when: `retriever ingest` builds a NON-ZERO-row index over the 2 PDFs (table nemo-retriever)
`retriever recall vdb-recall run --query-csv data/labeled_set.csv --table-name nemo-retriever` is used (NOT a plain `retriever query`)
Recall@1 / Recall@5 / Recall@10 are printed as numeric values for the labeled set
--table-name nemo-retriever is passed so recall reads the ingested table (not the nv-ingest default -> all-zero)
cases.known_caveat: Default-table mismatch: omitting --table-name makes recall read 'nv-ingest' (empty) and report Recall@k=0 for every query. The labeled-set page column is 1-indexed for the recall CSV.

cases.title: Add nDCG@k: same labeled set scored through the BEIR evaluator -> Recall@k AND nDCG@k
cases.complexity_axis: Adds exactly one dimension over rung 1: nDCG@k alongside Recall@k. The plain recall command prints Recall@k only; ranking quality (nDCG@k) is surfaced by the BEIR evaluator (compute_beir_metrics emits recall@k AND ndcg@k). Same tiny labeled set, now expressed as an annotations CSV (modality,query,answer,pdf,page) and scored via the bo767_csv loader so both metrics print together.
cases.prompt: Recall alone isn't enough -- I also want nDCG@k so I can see ranking quality, not just whether the right page is somewhere in the top-k. Score data/labeled_set.csv against the index and give me Recall@k and nDCG@k.
expected_commands: # nDCG@k path (BEIR evaluator over an annotations CSV; bo767_csv loader accepts a CSV path as dataset_name):

$RETRIEVER harness run --dataset data/labeled_set_annotations.csv --override evaluation_mode=beir --override beir_loader=bo767_csv --override lancedb_table=nemo-retriever --override beir_doc_id_field=pdf_page

expected_output.beir_block: BEIR run summary prints BOTH recall@k AND ndcg@k for ks (1,3,5,10): e.g. recall@5 ~ 1.0, ndcg@5 a float in [0,1] reflecting WHERE in the top-k the gold page landed (ndcg@1 == recall@1 when there is one relevant doc per query)
expected_output.note: compute_beir_metrics: ndcg@k = DCG(top-k relevances)/IDCG; with one relevant doc/query, a gold page at rank 1 gives ndcg contribution 1.0, at rank 2 gives 1/log2(3) ~ 0.63

pass_when: Recall@k is surfaced (rung-1 capability retained)
nDCG@k is ALSO surfaced as a number, for at least one k, over the same labeled set
both metrics come from the labeled set + index (the BEIR evaluator / bo767_csv loader path), not guessed
the eval reads the ingested table (lancedb_table=nemo-retriever)
cases.known_caveat: The bo767_csv loader does page_number = int(page)+1 (its `page` column is 0-indexed), so the annotations CSV variant must store P-1 to target 1-indexed page P. The plain recall command (rung 1) is 1-indexed -- the two CSV variants differ by this offset.

cases.title: Per-query breakdown: which queries passed/failed at which k
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the 'per-query breakdown' clause of the validation path over rung 2: not just aggregate Recall@k/nDCG@k, but a per-query table of hit@k (pass/fail) and rank@k. Produced by core.py::evaluate_recall (query_id, query, golden_answer, top_retrieved, hit@k, rank@k); `vdb-recall run --print-hits` also shows each query's Gold + retrieved hits inline so a human can see exactly which query missed.
cases.prompt: Don't just give me the average -- show me a per-query breakdown for data/labeled_set.csv: for each question, did the gold page land in the top-1 / top-5, and at what rank? I want to see exactly which queries passed and which failed.

expected_output.per_query: for each of the 3 queries: the Gold key ({pdf}_{page}) and the top-k retrieved keys, so hit@1 / hit@5 (pass/fail) and rank@k are derivable; evaluate_recall returns this as a DataFrame (hit@k bool + rank@k int/None per query)
expected_output.aggregate: the Recall@1/@5/@10 averages still print under the per-query lines
expected_output.example_breakdown: q1 'collection in 1923' -> gold woods_frost_2, expected hit@5=true; q2 'James 2019' -> gold table_test_1, hit@1 likely true (rank 1); q3 'miles to go' -> gold woods_frost_1, hit@1 likely true

pass_when: output includes a PER-QUERY view (each query's gold vs retrieved top-k, and/or hit@k pass/fail + rank@k), not only the aggregate
a reader can identify which specific query passed/failed at k=1 and k=5
aggregate Recall@k still surfaced
uses the recall sub-app over the labeled set (table nemo-retriever)

cases.title: Vary k (Recall@1 vs Recall@5) and surface a failing query
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the 'vary k / show a failing query' dimension over rung 3. A 5-query labeled set includes a deliberately harder query whose gold page does NOT land at rank 1 (so Recall@1 < Recall@5), making the k-sensitivity visible: the same system 'passes' at k=5 but 'fails' that query at k=1. Demonstrates Recall@1 vs Recall@5 as distinct numbers and pinpoints the failing query.
cases.prompt: Run the benchmark in data/labeled_set.csv and tell me how sensitive the result is to k: what's Recall@1 vs Recall@5, and which query is the one that only succeeds once I widen k (i.e. fails at k=1)?

expected_output.k_sensitivity: Recall@1 < Recall@5: at least one query has its gold page at rank > 1 (lands in top-5 but not top-1), so Recall@1 is strictly below Recall@5
expected_output.failing_query: the harder query ('List Robert Frost poetry collections and their publication years' -> gold woods_frost_2, the table-of-collections page) is the one whose gold page is NOT rank 1 but is within top-5; the breakdown shows hit@1=false, hit@5=true for it
expected_output.values: 5 queries; expected recall@5 = recall@10 = 1.0 (all gold pages within top-5), recall@1 < 1.0 (the harder query and/or a generic query miss rank 1)

pass_when: Recall@1 and Recall@5 are surfaced as DISTINCT numbers and Recall@1 < Recall@5
the specific failing-at-k=1 query is identified from the per-query breakdown
the same run shows a query that passes at k=5 but fails at k=1 (k-sensitivity demonstrated)
uses recall vdb-recall run over the 5-query labeled set (table nemo-retriever)
cases.known_caveat: Exact ranks depend on the embedder; the assertion is the QUALITATIVE shape (Recall@1 < Recall@5 with an identifiable failing query), not an exact float. A live run records the real per-query ranks.

cases.title: Acceptance: customer-style labeled set evaluated end-to-end -> Recall@k + nDCG@k + per-query breakdown, reproducible
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the EVALUATE row. Adds over rung 4: a CUSTOMER-STYLE labeled set (the bo767/digital_corpora annotations shape: modality,query,answer,pdf,page) evaluated END-TO-END -- ingest the corpus, run BOTH the Recall@k path and the BEIR Recall@k+nDCG@k path, produce the per-query breakdown, and show the numbers are REPRODUCIBLE across two runs on the same labeled set + index. This is the gate the others build up to.
cases.prompt: Here's a customer-style labeled set: data/customer_annotations.csv (modality, query, answer, pdf, page -- same shape as the bo767 / digital_corpora annotation files; a real slice is in data/bo767_annotations_slice.csv for reference). Build the index over the PDFs in data/ and give me a full retrieval-quality report: Recall@k and nDCG@k, a per-query breakdown of which questions passed/failed at which k, and confirm the numbers are reproducible if I run it again.
expected_commands: # BEIR path for Recall@k + nDCG@k together (bo767_csv loader accepts the CSV path as dataset_name):

$RETRIEVER harness run --dataset data/customer_annotations.csv --override evaluation_mode=beir --override beir_loader=bo767_csv --override lancedb_table=nemo-retriever --override beir_doc_id_field=pdf_page
# reproducibility check: re-run the recall command on the same index -> identical Recall@k

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (N >= 3; --use-table-structure so the James/Susan table cells are queryable)
expected_output.recall_block: Recall@1/@5/@10 as numbers over the 5-query customer labeled set
expected_output.beir_block: BEIR run summary prints Recall@k AND nDCG@k for ks (1,3,5,10) over the same labeled set
expected_output.per_query: per-query breakdown (hit@k pass/fail + rank@k, plus inline Gold vs top-k hits via --print-hits) covering all 5 customer questions
expected_output.reproducibility: two recall runs on the same index + labeled set produce IDENTICAL Recall@k values (deterministic embed + search)
expected_output.expected_values: with a correct index, the 5 gold pages are retrievable in top-5 -> recall@5 ~ 1.0; recall@1 < 1.0 if any gold page is not rank 1; nDCG@k between recall@1 and recall@5 reflecting ranking

pass_when: single end-to-end flow: ingest the corpus, then evaluate the customer-style labeled set
Recall@k surfaced as numbers (recall sub-app)
nDCG@k ALSO surfaced as numbers over the same labeled set (BEIR evaluator)
per-query breakdown present: which of the 5 questions passed/failed at which k
reproducibility shown: a second run on the same index + labeled set yields identical Recall@k
customer-style labeled-set format (modality,query,answer,pdf,page) consumed directly; --table-name nemo-retriever matches the ingest table
cases.known_caveat: Two label conventions in play: the recall command's CSV `page` is 1-indexed; the bo767_csv (BEIR) loader's `page` is 0-indexed (it adds 1). customer_annotations.csv is authored in the bo767 0-indexed convention for the BEIR/nDCG path; for the plain recall command the agent must account for the +1 (or supply a query,pdf,page CSV with 1-indexed pages). The real data/bo767_annotations.csv slice is included to document the production labeled-set shape.
